channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
item.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.
item.content:encoded: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: New strikes test the Iran ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.
item.content:encoded: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is being tested after new attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Mideast expert Paul Salem explains what's happened and whether the deal can still hold.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: What its like to report from an Ebola outbreak
item.description: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.
item.content:encoded: Covering an Ebola outbreak means taking extra precautions. In this Reporter's Notebook, NPR's Emmet Livingstone takes us behind the reporting in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Adam Raney

item.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
item.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."
item.content:encoded:
The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

(Image credit: Ashley Landis)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: A historian analyzes whether Ukraine has a localized advantage in modern combat power
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon talks with strategic studies professor Phillips O'Brien of St Andrew's University in Scotland about whether Ukraine has gained the upper hand in the fight against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Inside a secretive Ukrainian team launching deep drone strikes at Russia
item.description: Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.
item.content:encoded:
Ukraine's long-range drones are striking deep inside Russia, up to 1,200 miles away, hitting oil refineries and depots. NPR recently spent time with one of the Ukrainian strike teams launching drones at Russian targets.

(Image credit: Serhii Korovvayny for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Venezuelans in Colombia scramble to send aid as earthquakes death toll increases
item.description: Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed over 1,400 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.
item.content:encoded:
Aid efforts are intensifying after twin earthquakes killed over 1,400 in Venezuela, with international teams arriving but a slow government response hampering relief on the ground.

(Image credit: Manuel Reda for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: There's a beef about beef at the World Cup, as Argentina fans pour into Texas
item.description: It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.
item.content:encoded:
It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran each announce retaliatory strikes in Iran, Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: A recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran appears even more fragile following the latest hostilities in the region, which includes U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets.
item.content:encoded:
A recent ceasefire agreement between Washington and Tehran appears even more fragile following the latest hostilities in the region, which includes U.S. strikes on Iranian military targets.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
item.description: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.
item.content:encoded: Following the earthquakes in Venezuela, the Trump administration announced what appears to be its strongest disaster response since it dismantled America's premier aid agency USAID last year.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis
